matchups.tacticalAdvantage: Duke enters as the overall No. 1 seed after a 32-2 season. Despite late-season injuries to Caleb Foster, the talent gap anchored by Cameron Boozer is too wide for Siena to overcome. Duke fits the optimal AI Intellipick profile (No. 1 in KenPom) and opened as a massive -27.5 point betting favorite, perfectly aligning with the 99.3% historical win rate of 1-seeds.

TCU brings an elite defense (97.8 AdjD), a 75% cover rate as an underdog, and a massive coaching advantage. Sports Illustrated's Iain MacMillan officially picked TCU here, noting that Ohio State's 174th-ranked defensive efficiency is a glaring red flag against TCU's physical style.

While 12-seeds are trendy upset picks, Northern Iowa is utterly atrocious on the offensive glass (ranking 356th nationally). St. John's ranks 28th nationally in offensive rebounds. Action Network's Dr. Nick Giffen notes St. John's is vastly underseeded and actually picks them to reach the Final Four, validating their sturdy -10.5 point spread.

Kansas features potential top NBA pick Darryn Peterson. CBU is a fun story making its first NCAA tournament appearance in program history. The betting markets opened with Kansas as a comfortable -14.5 point favorite, aligning with the 79.5% historical win rate for 4-seeds.

Louisville is missing primary playmaker Mikel Brown Jr. due to a back injury. South Florida ranks second nationally in rebounds (43 per game). FOX Sports' Michael Cohen officially selected South Florida as his biggest first-round upset, citing USF's blistering pace against a depleted Louisville backcourt.

Michigan State extended its Big Ten record to 28 consecutive NCAA Tournament appearances. They share the ball exceptionally well and face a Summit League champion in NDSU. The Spartans opened as heavy -16.5 point favorites and perfectly fit the AI Intellipick criteria with top-tier efficiency metrics.

While UCF features an explosive offense, their defense fell off during Big 12 play. UCLA is getting healthy at the right time, heavily relying on the steady production of Tyler Bilodeau. Historical 7-seed dominance (60.3%) and UCLA's -5.5 point opening line make the Bruins the statistically sound pick.

Furman secured a No. 15 seed via the Southern Conference automatic bid and is a dangerous mid-major, but UConn under Dan Hurley is a dynastic mastermind with too much depth. The Huskies command a massive -19.5 point opening spread, fully supported by the 93.6% historical dominance of 2-seeds.

Arizona opened the season with 23 straight wins and is the model's projected national champion, featuring top-five ranks in both offensive and defensive efficiency. The Wildcats opened as tournament-high -29.5 to -31.5 point favorites.

Villanova is missing key rotation piece Matt Hodge and relies heavily on three-point variance. PoolGenius explicitly identified Utah State over Villanova as one of the best First-Round value picks, noting the Aggies are driven by their experienced backcourt and are actually a -1.5 point betting favorite despite the lower seed.

While High Point enters on a long winning streak, Wisconsin's battle-tested Big Ten pedigree provides a massive strength-of-schedule advantage. Avoiding the trendy 12-seed trap here aligns with the 64.7% historical win rate of 5-seeds, supporting Wisconsin's -11.5 point betting line.

Arkansas won the SEC Tournament, but sharp money heavily favors Hawai'i to cover the +15.5 point spread by exploiting Arkansas's defensive lapses. While the Rainbow Warriors are an attractive spread target, historical 4-seed dominance (79.5% win rate) tempers outright upset predictions, making Arkansas the mathematical pick.

Texas advanced to the Round of 64 after a gritty First Four win. BYU lost key rotation piece Richie Saunders to a season-ending ACL injury. PoolGenius highlights Texas as a massive value spot, noting that teams with First Four momentum historically outperform expectations, especially against a hobbled opponent.

Gonzaga secured a No. 3 seed and boasts elite efficiency. While considered less dominant than classic Bulldogs teams, historical 3-seed dominance (85.3%) and a heavy point spread support them comfortably advancing over Kennesaw State.

Missouri dropped to the 10-line late in the season after struggling down the stretch. PoolGenius identified this as a matchup where Miami's No. 31 KenPom rating comfortably edges out Missouri's No. 52 rating, aligning with historical data that favors the 7-seed 60.3% of the time.
Purdue enters at 27-8 after winning the Big Ten Tournament, facing tournament first-timer and Atlantic Sun champion Queens. Purdue's No. 8 KenPom rating makes them overwhelming -23.5 point favorites.

Howard advanced out of the First Four, but Michigan boasts the country's most efficient defense (No. 1 AdjD), anchored by 7-foot-7 rim protector Aday Mara. Michigan fits the perfect AI Intellipick profile, ensuring an immense talent mismatch.

8-vs-9 matchups are historical 50/50 toss-ups, but Georgia leads the nation in fast-break points and boasts an elite +3.09 turnover margin. Sharp money backs Georgia to exploit Saint Louis's 230th rank in offensive turnovers in a massive track meet.

Texas Tech lost All-American forward JT Toppin, forcing them into a predictable offense. Akron ranks in the top fifteen nationally in shooting percentage. FanSided experts correctly point out that Texas Tech is ripe for an early exit given their battered roster, providing Akron a massive opening.

While Hofstra's slow pace is dangerous, Alabama's No. 18 KenPom ranking and volume three-point shooting make them statistically secure. 13-seeds only win 20.5% of the time, making Alabama the safe mathematical pick to advance.

Miami (OH) built a 31-1 record on a weak schedule. While 11-seeds are trendy upset picks, Tennessee is battle-tested, healthy, and will overwhelm Miami with SEC-tier speed and a 44.7% offensive rebounding rate.

Virginia secured a No. 3 seed with their trademark pack-line defense and will look to suffocate the Horizon League champion. Virginia qualifies as a fringe AI Intellipick with elite efficiency numbers, driving their status as -17.5 point favorites.

Kentucky struggles to score in half-court sets and is missing point guard Jaland Lowe. Sports Illustrated published that 'Santa Clara over Kentucky will be one of the most common upset picks,' noting the Wildcats' vulnerability to Santa Clara's 29.2 three-point attempts per game. The betting line opened remarkably tight with Kentucky favored by only -3.5 points.

Iowa State relies on interior dominance and boasts incredible defensive metrics. Tennessee State represents the Ohio Valley Conference auto-bid. Iowa State matches the exact AI Intellipick criteria with elite defense, commanding a massive -23.5 spread.

Florida is the defending national champion and won the SEC regular-season title. The massive talent gap perfectly aligns with the 99.3% historical win rate of 1-seeds. Florida is an elite No. 4 in KenPom, assuring a blowout victory.

8-vs-9 matchups are notoriously close. Iowa brings strong March experience and is the slight betting favorite, heavily influenced by Clemson missing key rebounder Carter Welling to a torn ACL. A Reddit mega-guide specifically targeted Iowa advancing as a high-confidence metric play.

Vanderbilt is severely underseeded. PoolGenius noted that Vanderbilt's perimeter shooting and physical forwards are perfectly equipped to exploit McNeese's poor defensive rebounding. Oddsmakers agree, opening Vandy as a strong -10.5 point favorite and safely avoiding the 12-over-5 trap.

Nebraska heavily regressed late in the season, and Troy limits shot efficiency at the basket. While Troy is highly live to cover the 13.5-point spread, predicting an outright upset here requires tempering expectations given the 79.5% historical win rate of 4-seeds.

UNC lost interior enforcer Caleb Wilson to a season-ending hand fracture. Action Network's Dr. Nick Giffen highlighted VCU over UNC as his primary 'leverage spot' for brackets. PoolGenius also listed VCU as one of the strongest First-Round upset opportunities, noting UNC's late-season regression.

Illinois possesses the country's most productive attack. Penn arrives hot off an overtime thriller to win the Ivy League tournament under coach Fran McCaffery, but faces a massive talent deficit. Illinois sits at No. 7 in KenPom, justifying their enormous -20.5 point spread.

A heavy stylistic clash: Saint Mary's dictates a slow, defensive slog and leads the nation with an 81% free-throw rate, while Texas A&M pushes a fast track meet. 7-seeds win roughly 60% of the time, aligning with Saint Mary's narrow odds, making them the safer mathematical play.

Houston was placed in the South Region, setting up a highly motivated run toward a potential regional final in their hometown at the Toyota Center. Houston ranks No. 5 in KenPom and supports a massive -19.5 point spread.
